Option3: cariformes are mites. Dust mites are often found in house dust, and are responsible for allergies in some people. Chiggers are a type of mite whose parasitical larvae feed on animal skin cells, and can cause intense itching and irritation.
Option4: mblypygi are often called whip-spiders or tailless whip-scorpions. Their front pair of legs are modified to be used as sensory organs, rather than for locomotion, and can be as much as five times as long as the body.
